title: Candollea assimilis
taxonomy: Candollea assimilis is classified within the family Santalaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sandalwood or mistletoe family. The genus Candollea belongs to the order Santalales, which is a group of flowering plants that are typically parasitic or hemiparasitic. Candollea assimilis is a species of flowering plant endemic to Australia. It is a rare species found in the tropical regions of the country. The classification of Candollea assimilis is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationship with other plants in the Santalaceae family.
